title: Guy Ritchie and the Challenges of Making 'King Arthur' | The Big Picture (Ep. 12)
format: Sean Fennessey and Chris Ryan trace Guy Ritchie's career up to 'King Arthur: Legend of the Sword,' then Ritchie joins to explain cutting the film from three and a half hours down to two.

segments.heading: Guy Ritchie, from cockney gangsters to Camelot

films.title: King Arthur: Legend of the Sword
films.note: The new film: an origin story that strips out Merlin, Guinevere and the Grail and plays like Lock, Stock with a sword in a stone.

films.title: Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels
films.note: Ritchie's debut, made small and independent, with Jason Statham early in his career.

films.title: Snatch
films.note: The wider-seen follow-up that put him on the map. Ryan calls it the Hollywood version of Lock, Stock.

films.title: Revolver
films.note: The downturn: Ryan finds it incomprehensible and short on the earlier films' energy.

films.title: RocknRolla
films.note: Fun to watch but where his ability to evolve past the crime template hits a ceiling.

films.title: Sherlock Holmes
films.note: The franchise renaissance with Robert Downey Jr. and Jude Law, a big hit.

films.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows
The Man from U.N.C.L.E.
films.note: A cult favorite in hindsight, with Henry Cavill and Armie Hammer.

films.title: Excalibur
films.note: John Boorman's film. Ryan's pick for the one genuinely good King Arthur movie, because it commits to the romance of the myth.

films.title: King Arthur
films.note: Antoine Fuqua's historical-realist take with Clive Owen and Keira Knightley as a Celtic warrior Guinevere.

films.title: King Arthur: Legend of the Sword
films.note: Frames it as a man's struggle with himself, with Arthur as the alibi; shot a three-and-a-half-hour cut, then squidged $20M of scenes down to under two hours to keep the momentum.

films.title: Excalibur
films.note: Boorman's film affected him deeply, but he saw its second half congest under too many components (Lancelot, Guinevere, Morgana, Merlin) and set out to strip them away.
The reference point for the epic-fantasy license: 'we got 300 for elephants.'

films.title: Django Unchained
films.note: Held up as a very confident piece of work, the kind of filmmaking that excites him.

films.title: Whiplash
films.note: He says he never sat down watching it, because of the confidence of the expression.

films.title: Aladdin
films.note: His next film for Disney: a street kid who becomes a king, and an excuse to make a musical after scoring King Arthur.

King Arthur: Legend of the Sword.title: King Arthur: Legend of the Sword

Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels.title: Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels

Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows
